channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Nísia Trindade se filia ao PT e concorrerá à Câmara pelo Rio
item.description: Former Health Minister Nísia Trindade has joined the Workers' Party (PT) and will run for the Chamber of Deputies from Rio de Janeiro. Her campaign will focus on defending science and vaccination, highlighting her leadership at Fiocruz during the Covid-19 pandemic. She left office in February 2025 as part of a ministerial reshuffle in the Lula government.

item.title: Aliado de Castro promete ser candidato caso STF decida por eleição direta
item.description: State deputy Douglas Ruas (PL) stated he would run for governor of Rio de Janeiro if the STF decides on an early direct election. Supported by former governor Cláudio Castro (PL), Ruas is already a pre-candidate of his party for the state race.

item.title: Pimenta fala em “conexão direta” entre fraude do INSS e “BolsoMaster”
item.description: Deputy Paulo Pimenta (PT-RS) stated there is a 'direct connection' between INSS fraud, the Banco Master case, and Jair Bolsonaro's government, dubbing the scandal 'BolsoMaster'. He advocates for Bolsonaro's indictment on charges of organized crime, qualified theft against the elderly, and administrative impropriety, based on an alternative CPMI-INSS report calling for the indictment of 130 individuals, including the former president and his son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Kataguiri entra com ação contra governo Lula por disparos de WhatsApp
item.description: Deputy Kim Kataguiri (Missão-SP) filed a popular action against the Lula government in São Paulo’s judiciary, claiming that WhatsApp message blasts promoting the Gás do Povo program illegally used private companies instead of the official government app and reached non-beneficiaries. He requests an immediate halt to the mass messages, blocking of payments to contracted firms, and a daily fine of at least R$ 100,000 for non-compliance. The program, launched in September 2025, covers nearly 15 million families and is a key part of Lula’s 2026 electoral strategy.

item.title: PF prende Rodrigo Bacellar, ex-presidente da Alerj
item.description: Former state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar (União), ex-president of the Alerj, was preventively arrested by the STF in Teresópolis for obstructing a PF investigation into the Red Command. The arrest stems from Operation Unha e Carne, linked to Operation Zargun, where Bacellar allegedly tipped off TH Joias about police raids and shared information with Judge Macário Júdice Neto, who was removed for leaking confidential data. Bacellar had his mandate revoked by the TSE in 2024 for abuse of power in the 2022 elections.

item.title: CPMI do INSS chega ao fim com pedidos de indiciamento e investigação de ministros
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Commission (CPMI) released its final report, calling for the indictment of four lawmakers, former ministers from President Lula's government, and Lula’s son, Fábio Lula da Silva (Lulinha). The report also notes the upcoming election to define Rio de Janeiro’s interim governor and the house arrest of former President Jair Bolsonaro following hospitalization. The investigation centers on alleged irregularities in the management of the social security institute.

item.title: Vice da CLDF pede ao TCDF suspensão de patrocínio do BRB a sala VIP
item.description: The vice-president of the Legislative Chamber of the Federal District, Ricardo Vale (PT), has asked the TCDF to suspend BRB’s sponsorship of the VIP lounge at Brasília International Airport, worth R$58.3 million, citing alleged mismanagement and financial instability at the bank. The 36-month contract, effective from March 2026, grants access only to high-income clients (DUX, black, and infinite tiers). BRB maintains it has the financial capacity to support the investment, which aligns with its strategy to strengthen its portfolio and brand positioning.

item.title: A disputa de narrativas entre direita e esquerda sobre a guerra no Irã
item.description: The Iran war, now one month old, has become a battleground of narratives between Brazil’s political right and left, with each side using the conflict to advance ideological agendas. The right supports U.S. actions, citing Iranian regime violence, while the left links the war to Netanyahu and Trump’s interests, including Epstein-related conspiracy theories. The Lupa Observatory notes that unreliable data fuels speculation and AI-generated disinformation, including misleading images like a falsely contextualized photo of Vice President Alckmin with a Hamas leader.

item.title: Moraes arquiva pedido de investigação contra governador Casagrande por troca de mensagens com Macário
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes archived the criminal complaint against Espírito Santo Governor Renato Casagrande over messages with Judge Macário Júdice Neto, arrested for alleged influence-peddling and selling rulings. The Federal Police had requested an investigation based on chats suggesting favor exchanges, but Moraes found no minimal evidence of criminal wrongdoing. Casagrande said the ruling confirms his claim that the conversations were strictly institutional and republican.

item.title: Após 7 anos, Goiás aprova aumento de pensões às vítimas do Césio-137
item.description: After seven years without adjustment, the Goiás Legislative Assembly approved an increase in pensions for victims of the Cesium-137 accident, raising payments from R$954 to R$1,621 for most beneficiaries and from R$1,908 to R$3,242 for those with direct exposure. The bill, sent by the state executive on March 17, now awaits sanction by Governor Ronaldo Caiado to take effect. Approximately 603 people currently receive the lifelong pension linked to the 1987 radiological disaster.

item.title: Kassab indica que Felício deixará PSD para ficar como vice de Tarcísio
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab informed Governor Tarcísio de Freitas that Vice Governor Felício Ramuth will leave the party if he remains on the reelection ticket, opening the door for a possible move to the MDB. The decision follows months of tension, with Kassab seeking to replace Ramuth as the gubernatorial running mate. Despite the rift, Kassab publicly stated that the PSD supports Tarcísio and that Ramuth’s party status will be discussed after April 4.

item.title: PT inclui Flávio Bolsonaro em lista de indiciados da CPI do INSS
item.description: The PT presented an alternative report to the INSS CPMI with 201 indicted individuals, including Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for President. The report, by deputy Rogério Correia (PT-MG), criticizes the official opinion as politically motivated and for excluding figures like Fabiano Zettel, son-in-law of banker Daniel Vorcaro. The PT claims evidence links Flávio Bolsonaro to financial schemes involving Banco Master, including a R$1 billion bailout, and urges the PF to investigate around 50 people further.

item.title: Volta de Bolsonaro agita moradores do condomínio
item.description: The Solar de Brasília condominium, residence of Jair Bolsonaro, distributed an internal notice to residents with coexistence and security rules due to the former president's house arrest. The measure was taken following a decision by the Supreme Federal Court, causing agitation among residents. The notice aims to ensure order and safety in the building amid Bolsonaro's presence.

item.title: Moraes arquiva pedido de investigação contra Casagrande por mensagens com desembargador
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes archived the Federal Police's request for an inquiry against former Espírito Santo governor Renato Casagrande, concerning messages exchanged with Judge Macário Judice, arrested in the TH Joias case. Moraes determined the messages showed no minimal evidence of criminal wrongdoing and lacked proof of administrative advocacy with intent, emphasizing that investigations without just cause constitute illegal coercion.

item.title: Vamos ter maioria no Senado e 'impichar' Alexandre de Moraes, diz Eduardo Bolsonaro nos EUA
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro stated in a CPAC speech in the U.S. that if Senator Flávio Bolsonaro is elected, Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes will face an impeachment process. The remark is part of a political strategy to pressure the Judiciary and mobilize the conservative base.

item.title: 2ª ação no STF contra Moro por insinuação sobre a eleição de 2022 fica com Kassio
item.description: Minister Kassio Nunes Marques was appointed as rapporteur of a STF action investigating Senator Sergio Moro’s unsubstantiated claims about the legitimacy of the 2022 election. Federal Deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) filed the petition, arguing that Moro’s allegations spread symbolic repertoires of electoral fraud with present and future consequences. In a related inquiry, Minister Alexandre de Moraes had already requested input from the Prosecutor General’s Office.

item.title: Queridinho do PL x aliado de Lula: quem devem ser os candidatos ao governo-tampão no Rio
item.description: Two names stand out as leading candidates for the interim government in Rio de Janeiro following Claudio Castro's removal: Douglas Ruas (PL), former Cities Secretary and current President of the State Legislature, backed by the ruling coalition, and André Ceciliano (PT), former Alerj President and ex-Lula official, supported by the opposition. The STF has ruled for a secret ballot in the snap election, which benefits opposition negotiations; they are also backing Chico Machado (Solidariedade) for the Alerj presidency to potentially form a winning tandem with Ceciliano.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: governo exonera ministro da Agricultura para tirar voto da oposição em dia decisivo
item.description: President Lula sacked Agriculture Minister Carlos Fávaro so he could return to the Senate and prevent opposition senator Margareth Buzetti (PP-MT) from remaining a titular member of the INSS CPMI, whose report calls for the preventive arrest of Lulinha, the president’s son. The move aims to secure the report’s rejection before the commission’s deadline, after the STF denied its extension. Buzetti accused the government of disrespect and of trying to shield Lulinha through political maneuvering.

item.title: Eduardo na CPAC: 'Vamos ganhar eleição, anistiar Jair e impichar Moraes'
item.description: Former federal deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro, brother of ex-president Jair Bolsonaro, spoke at CPAC in the U.S., declaring intentions to 'win the election, pardon Jair, and impeach Moraes'. He presented his brother Flávio as 'the next president of Brazil' and noted being applauded by his older brother — a meaningful moment after living over a year in the United States.

item.title: Pedro Rousseff pede a Moraes a revogação da prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Belo Horizonte city councilor Pedro Rousseff (PT) asked Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes to revoke Jair Bolsonaro's house arrest, claiming the former president is using the measure as a platform for political activity, citing statements by Eduardo Bolsonaro about political discussions with visitors.

item.title: EUA avaliam designar PCC e CV terroristas por “pressão dos Bolsonaros”, diz NYT
item.description: The United States is considering designating the PCC and CV as foreign terrorist organizations, allegedly due to pressure from the Bolsonaros, according to The New York Times. Such a designation could benefit Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), a pre-candidate for president in 2026, by reinforcing his hardline stance on organized crime.

item.title: "Vai ter velho chorando": as reações de vizinhos com a volta de Bolsonaro a condomínio
item.description: Residents of the Solar de Brasília II condominium expressed dissatisfaction with the return of former President Jair Bolsonaro, who will serve his sentence under house arrest after being convicted by the STF. In WhatsApp group messages, neighbors complained about the loss of peace, describing it as a 'true hell' and recalling past incidents involving sound trucks and protests. The condominium administration issued guidelines on rules to be followed during Bolsonaro’s stay.

item.title: EUA deve taxar PCC e CV como terroristas após pressão do clã Bolsonaro, diz NYT
item.description: The U.S. government is considering designating Brazil's PCC and CV criminal factions as terrorist groups following pressure from the Bolsonaro family, particularly Flávio and Eduardo Bolsonaro, who engaged with U.S. diplomats. Brazil's Lula administration opposes the move, citing legal and sovereignty concerns, but says it is open to transnational cooperation against organized crime.

item.title: Fávaro é exonerado do cargo de ministro para votar relatório da CPMI
item.description: Agriculture Minister Carlos Fávaro was removed from office to return to the Senate and vote on the INSS CPMI report, which calls for the indictment of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha) on charges including money laundering and organized crime. The decision by President Lula was made without prior notice to his alternate, Senator Margareth Buzetti, who expressed regret. The government aims to strengthen its position in the committee while preparing an alternative report calling for the indictment of Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Moraes rejeita pedido de inquérito contra Renato Casagrande por relação com juiz federal
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes dismissed the Federal Police's request to open an investigation against Espírito Santo Governor Renato Casagrande (PSB) over alleged improper ties with a federal judge. The ruling, issued on Friday, March 27, 2026, closes the preliminary inquiry into the matter.

item.title: Chegada de Moro ao PL do Paraná provoca debandada de prefeitos
item.description: The arrival of Sergio Moro to the PL party in Paraná triggered the departure of 48 out of 53 municipal mayors affiliated with the party, led by federal deputy Fernando Giacobo, in protest against the breach of a prior agreement to support the PSD in the 2026 elections. The mayors state they wish to maintain their alliance with Governor Ratinho Jr., whom they describe as a 'municipalist governor,' and reject the party's new direction following Moro's affiliation, which aims to support Flávio Bolsonaro's presidential bid.

item.title: Planalto aponta principal vilão que pode prejudicar Lula em próximas pesquisas
item.description: Lula's allies identify rising fuel prices as the main threat to his future polling numbers, potentially allowing Flávio Bolsonaro to surpass him. However, the PT campaign believes Lula is already responding with solutions, the race is still early, and Flávio will face wear-and-tear once past scandals are brought to light.

item.title: Governo reage ŕ inclusăo de Lulinha na CPI do INSS e pede indiciamento de Bolsonaro e Flávio
item.description: The government's bloc in the mixed INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPI) will propose the indictment of former President Jair Bolsonaro and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, in reaction to the inclusion of Lulinha (Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva Filho) in the investigation. The move seeks to counter allegations against the Lula family by requesting accountability for the Bolsonaro family over alleged irregularities in the INSS.

item.title: CPMI: governistas reagem e incluem Flávio em relatório alternativo
item.description: Government-aligned lawmakers in the INSS CPMI decided to include Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) in the commission's alternative final report as a reaction to the indictment request against Lulinha by rapporteur Alfredo Gaspar. The inclusion is based on allegations of alleged influence peddling involving Rio's pension fund (Rioprevidência), with claims that Flávio influenced the transfer of R$1 billion to Master. The alternative report, led by Deputy Paulo Pimenta (PT-RS), aims to mobilize the government base for voting starting Friday, March 27, possibly extending into the weekend.

item.title: Clica e Confirma ressalta atuação da Ouvidoria da Mulher
item.description: This week's Clica e Confirma program highlighted the work of the TSE's Women's Ombudsman, which recorded 37,565 demands in 2025, 114 of which were related to women's issues. It also covered a meeting between TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia, the presidents of the Regional Electoral Tribunals (TREs), and the Federal Police command to define the security strategy for the 2026 Elections, along with reporting on the ineligibility of former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro and the April 3 deadline for the party window.

item.title: Base de Lula quer pedido de indiciamento de Flávio Bolsonaro e Onyx Lorenzoni na CPI do INSS
item.description: PT deputies announced they will propose the indictment of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro and former minister Onyx Lorenzoni in the INSS CPI, rejecting the rapporteur's report that sought the indictment of Lulinha, President Lula's eldest son. They argue the report is politically motivated and ignores evidence against Bolsonaro allies, such as Fabiano Zettel, brother-in-law of Banco Master's owner, and Letícia Caetano dos Reis, Flávio Bolsonaro's law firm partner. Senator Margareth Buzetti denounced being forcibly removed from the CPI by Carlos Fávaro, the Agriculture Minister, who seeks to reclaim his Senate seat.

item.title: STF forma maioria por voto secreto na eleição indireta do RJ
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) formed a majority to rule that the indirect election for Rio de Janeiro’s governor will be conducted by secret vote in the State Legislative Assembly (Alerj), requiring public officials to resign within 24 hours to run. Relator Luiz Fux supported secret voting but advocated a 6-month resignation period, while Alexandre de Moraes and Gilmar Mendes favored direct elections. Justices may amend their requests until March 31, 2026.

item.title: Renan cresce na direita e acende alerta no QG de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Renan Santos, pre-candidate for the Missão party, is gaining traction on the right by attacking Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) over his 'bonecão do posto' dance, which he called a 'complete mental retard' in a post with nearly one million views on X. Flávio’s campaign is considering strategies such as keeping Romeu Zema (Novo) as a presidential candidate and Aldo Rebelo (DC) as an alternative to counter Renan’s rise, as he targets right-wing voters dissatisfied with Flávio’s moderate tone.

item.title: Pré-candidata ao Senado, Michelle diz que renuncia a ‘qualquer coisa’ para cuidar de Bolsonaro
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro, pre-candidate for Senate from the PL party, announced she is renouncing any political activity to care for her husband, Jair Bolsonaro, following his hospital discharge and house arrest granted by the STF. She stated her priority is her family and that they will live 'one day at a time,' while Bolsonaro serves a 27-year and three-month sentence for attempted coup and organized crime.

item.title: CPI do INSS mira Toffoli, mulher de Moraes e filho de Lewandowski
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS CPMI recommended investigating alleged ties between Banco Master owner Daniel Vorcaro and Supreme Court ministers Dias Toffoli and Alexandre de Moraes, as well as the son of former minister Ricardo Lewandowski. Suspicions include illicit practices in consigned credit operations, influence peddling, and improper benefits, though no ministers or relatives have been indicted so far.

item.title: Moraes, Dino, Gilmar e Zanin defendem eleições diretas para escolha de governador-tempão no Rio
item.description: Four STF ministers (Moraes, Dino, Gilmar, and Zanin) advocated for direct elections to choose an interim governor for Rio de Janeiro after Claudio Castro's resignation. However, six ministers currently support an indirect election via the state legislature, leading to a 6-4 vote in favor of indirect elections. The virtual trial continues until Monday, and positions may still change.

item.title: Lula deve finalmente procurar Davi Alcolumbre para tratar de Messias
item.description: Allies of President Lula believe he should seek out Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to unblock the analysis of Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF. After avoiding sending the presidential message due to fear of Senate defeat, Lula now aims to reconcile with Alcolumbre before formalizing the nomination, also seeking to realign the government with the Amapaense senator ahead of the 2026 electoral cycle.

item.title: EUA avaliam taxar CV e PCC como terroristas após pressão dos Bolsonaro, diz ‘NYT’
item.description: The U.S. government is considering designating Brazil’s Comando Vermelho (CV) and Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC) as terrorist organizations, following lobbying by Bolsonaro’s sons, Flávio and Eduardo, who see the move as a boost for Flávio’s 2026 presidential campaign. The proposal, under review by the Trump administration, is viewed by the Brazilian government as an attempt at foreign interference, particularly after prior sanctions were applied to Brazilian Supreme Court justices.

item.title: STF forma maioria por eleição indireta e votação secreta para o governo do RJ
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) formed a majority to maintain indirect election and secret voting in the Rio de Janeiro State Assembly (Alerj) to choose the new state governor. State deputies will decide the successor by October without being required to disclose their votes. The decision upholds the current constitutional process for filling a gubernatorial vacancy.

item.title: Bolsonaro usa os filhos de pombo-correio, diz Pedro Rousseff
item.description: BH city councilman Pedro Rousseff (PT) accused former President Jair Bolsonaro of using his house arrest as a cover to continue political activity, particularly through his sons, citing public statements by Eduardo Bolsonaro. Rousseff urged the Supreme Federal Court to revoke the measure, arguing it constitutes an abuse of power and judicial benefit instrumentalization for electoral purposes in 2026.

item.title: Governo Tarcísio endurece regra e passa a exigir 40% de desconto em precatórios
item.description: The government of Tarcísio de Freitas has instituted a flat 40% discount for direct precatory settlements in São Paulo, replacing the previous progressive discount system. The measure aims to reduce judicial liabilities amid a public deficit exceeding R$ 12 billion, with exceptions for priority creditors (elderly, people with disabilities, and those with serious illnesses), who retain a 20% discount. The state's precatory stock exceeds R$ 30 billion.

item.title: O futuro na Câmara do projeto de lei que pune misoginia no país
item.description: The bill criminalizing misogyny as a crime equivalent to racism faces strong resistance in the Chamber of Deputies, despite advancing in the Senate with support from Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. Chamber President Hugo Motta signaled he will handle the bill’s progress cautiously and without rush, avoiding an urgency request to prevent igniting political controversy.

item.title: Ouvidoria em Foco: página na internet está mais acessível e transparente
item.description: The Electoral Justice Ombudsman’s Office (TSE) launched a new, more accessible and transparent webpage to improve access to electoral services and information. The update, based on user feedback from late 2025, enhances navigation and prioritizes frequently requested topics, particularly around key electoral periods such as first-time voter registration and absentee voting.

item.title: Crusoé: Em busca de um vice
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) is already defining his potential running mate for the 2026 presidential ticket, even before the electoral incompatibility period ends. The leading candidates are former Minas Gerais governor Romeu Zema (Novo) and former minister and senator Tereza Cristina (MS), both aligned with conservative and liberal factions. The choice aims to balance regional appeal, administrative experience, and alliances with emerging political forces.

item.title: Economistas que consolidaram Plano Real querem Leite na Presidência
item.description: Economists who helped consolidate the Plano Real, Pérsio Arida and Armínio Fraga, have endorsed Eduardo Leite (PSD) as a presidential pre-candidate for 2026, citing his ability to lead Brazil in a less polarized environment. With Ratinho Junior withdrawing from the PSD internal race, the party’s national president, Gilberto Kassab, must decide the candidate by March 31, leaving Leite and Ronaldo Caiado as the remaining contenders.

item.title: STF tem maioria para votação secreta em eleição indireta para o governo do Rio
item.description: The Supreme Court (STF) formed a majority to hold the indirect election for Rio de Janeiro’s governor in secret, with six ministers voting in favor. Alexandre de Moraes and Gilmar Mendes dissented, advocating for a direct election. No majority has yet been formed on the 24-hour incompatibilization deadline, which currently stands at 5 to 1 in favor of 24 hours, while Luiz Fux argues for six months.

item.title: Relatório cita mesada do Careca do INSS para Lulinha
item.description: The final report of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI), presented by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar, requested the indictment of Lulinha (Fábio Luís Lula da Silva), son of President Lula, for alleged involvement in a fraud scheme involving 'Careca do INSS', Antonio Carlos Camilo Antunes. The document cites a PF deposition indicating R$ 25 million in payments and a monthly R$ 300 thousand payment from Careca to Lulinha, mediated by lobbyist Roberta Luchsinger, along with accusations of using public funds for first-class international flights and luxury accommodations.

item.title: As reações com a volta de Bolsonaro para condomínio: "Fim do sossego"
item.description: The return of former President Jair Bolsonaro to the Solar de Brasília II condominium to serve house arrest sparked negative reactions from neighbors, who complained about the end of peace and feared a return of loud protests. Residents expressed dissatisfaction in WhatsApp groups, describing the situation as a 'true hell' with sound trucks and symbolic support. While a few mentioned positive aspects, such as the presence of trumpeter Fabiano e Silva Leitão Duarte, the majority viewed his return as a disturbance.

item.title: Eleição no Rio será indireta e com voto fechado, decide STF
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) ruled that Rio de Janeiro's elections will be indirect, held in the State Legislative Assembly, with secret voting and a 24-hour desincompatibilization period. The decision partially satisfies both the opposition, which seeks to avoid open party defections, and Castro’s base, which wanted to prevent former officials from being barred by a longer disqualification period. Douglas Ruas and Nicola Miccione, recently removed from office, are seen as front-runners in the indirect race.

item.title: STF tem maioria para rejeitar ações que questionam privatização da Sabesp
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court formed a majority to reject two lawsuits challenging the privatization of Sabesp, filed by PT, PSOL, Rede, PV and PCdoB. Relator Minister Cristiano Zanin argued the claims are generic and do not contest specific constitutional points, and that the matter should have been addressed in São Paulo’s state court. The virtual session concluded with votes in favor of rejection from Zanin, Gilmar Mendes, Cármen Lúcia, Dias Toffoli, Edson Fachin and Alexandre de Moraes.
